ai_justification: Auto-selected fallback: 'late_delivery_risk' chosen from actual dataset columns.
ai_feature_strategy: Create ratio and interaction features between numeric variables.
ai_insights: Dataset has 180,519 rows × 53 columns. Target auto-detected as 'late_delivery_risk'.
hypothesis_insights: The business should either avoid offering 1-2 day shipping options or invest heavily in expedited logistics capabilities, as current operations cannot reliably meet tight delivery windows with over 95% of 1-day shipments arriving late.

The business should prioritize reducing shipping delays as even a 1-day delay is strongly associated with late delivery risk, suggesting tight scheduling or the need for buffer time in delivery promises.

The business should prioritize resource allocation and handling procedures for PAYMENT, DEBIT, and CASH transactions which have 15-18% higher late delivery rates compared to TRANSFER transactions, potentially indicating these types require more complex processing or face operational bottlenecks.

The business should prioritize improving logistics and inventory management for high-risk categories like Golf Bags & Carts, Lacrosse, and Pet Supplies to reduce late deliveries and improve customer satisfaction.

The business should investigate operational processes in Pet Shop and Book Shop departments to identify bottlenecks and implement best practices from better-performing departments like Footwear and Discs Shop to reduce overall late delivery risk.

The business should consider implementing priority fulfillment processes for higher-value orders while investigating whether resource constraints are causing lower-benefit orders to be deprioritized, leading to increased delivery delays.
true_hypotheses: Orders with lower scheduled shipping days (days_for_shipment_scheduled) tend to have higher late_delivery_risk due to tighter delivery windows
Orders where actual shipping days exceed scheduled days tend to have higher late_delivery_risk
Orders of specific transaction types (type) tend to have higher late_delivery_risk due to different handling requirements
Orders in specific product categories (category_name) tend to have higher late_delivery_risk due to specialized handling or inventory issues
Orders from specific departments (department_name) tend to have higher late_delivery_risk due to operational efficiency differences
Orders with lower benefit per order (benefit_per_order) tend to have slightly higher late_delivery_risk due to lower prioritization in fulfillment
